levels.label: Debutant
levels.description: Knows Java syntax, primitive types and simple control flow.

topics.name: Primitive types
attendus: Understand int, double, boolean and char

exemple_question.enonce: Which Java type is intended to store true or false values?
exemple_question.explication: The boolean type stores only true or false.

exemple_question.enonce: How many times does this loop execute? for (int i = 0; i < 3; i++) { }
exemple_question.explication: The loop runs for i = 0, 1 and 2, so it executes 3 times.

exemple_question.enonce: Which keyword returns a value from a Java method?
exemple_question.explication: The return keyword sends a value back to the caller.

exemple_question.enonce: What is the result of "Hello" + " " + "Java" ?
exemple_question.explication: String concatenation preserves the space in the middle.

exemple_question.enonce: How do you access the first element of an int[] named numbers?
exemple_question.explication: Java arrays are indexed with square brackets.

levels.label: Novice
levels.description: Uses arrays, lists, classes and exceptions in common situations.

exemple_question.enonce: Which method adds an element to an ArrayList<String> names?
exemple_question.explication: ArrayList uses add to append an element.

exemple_question.enonce: Which keyword creates a new instance of a class in Java?
exemple_question.explication: The new keyword creates an object instance.

exemple_question.enonce: Which access modifier restricts a field to its declaring class?
exemple_question.explication: private makes the field accessible only inside its class.

exemple_question.enonce: What is the purpose of a catch block in Java?
exemple_question.explication: A catch block handles an exception raised in the associated try block.

exemple_question.enonce: Which statement about a constructor is correct?
exemple_question.choix_B: It has the same name as the class
exemple_question.choix_C: It can be called without new
exemple_question.explication: A constructor has the same name as its class and no return type.

levels.label: Intermediaire
levels.description: Uses generics, streams, interfaces and object equality correctly.

exemple_question.enonce: What does List<String> mean in Java?
exemple_question.choix_A: A list that accepts any type
exemple_question.choix_B: A list restricted to String values
exemple_question.explication: List<String> restricts the list to String values.

exemple_question.enonce: Which stream operation keeps only the elements matching a predicate?
exemple_question.explication: filter retains only the elements that satisfy the predicate.

topics.name: equals and hashCode
attendus: Know when to override equals and hashCode

exemple_question.enonce: Why should hashCode usually be overridden when equals is overridden?
exemple_question.choix_B: To preserve the contract of hash-based collections
exemple_question.explication: HashMap and HashSet rely on the equals/hashCode contract.

exemple_question.enonce: Which keyword associates a class with an interface it fulfills?
exemple_question.explication: A class uses implements to fulfill an interface contract.

exemple_question.enonce: Which Optional method provides a default value when the Optional is empty?
exemple_question.explication: orElse returns the contained value or a default value when empty.

levels.label: Avance
levels.description: Writes robust backend code, tests it, and reasons about concurrency and transactions.

topics.name: CompletableFuture
attendus: Choose the right abstraction for async work

exemple_question.enonce: Which Java type is designed to represent an asynchronous computation that may produce a value later?
exemple_question.explication: CompletableFuture models an asynchronous computation and its result.

exemple_question.enonce: What does a rollback do in a transaction?
exemple_question.explication: Rollback cancels the changes that were not committed.

exemple_question.enonce: What is the main goal of a unit test?
exemple_question.choix_A: Test the entire production system end to end
exemple_question.choix_B: Verify a small isolated behavior of the code
exemple_question.explication: A unit test targets a narrow, isolated behavior.

exemple_question.enonce: Where are Java objects primarily allocated at runtime?
exemple_question.explication: Objects are primarily allocated on the heap.

exemple_question.enonce: Which keyword helps make a field non-reassignable after initialization?
exemple_question.explication: final prevents a field from being reassigned after initialization.

levels.label: Expert
levels.description: Masters JVM internals, performance, architecture and advanced design trade-offs.

exemple_question.enonce: Which JVM component compiles hot bytecode paths into optimized native code at runtime?
exemple_question.explication: The JIT compiler optimizes hot code paths at runtime.

topics.name: Garbage collection
attendus: Understand the purpose of the GC

exemple_question.enonce: What is the primary role of the Java garbage collector?
exemple_question.choix_B: Free memory used by unreachable objects
exemple_question.explication: The garbage collector reclaims memory from objects that are no longer reachable.

exemple_question.enonce: Which pattern encapsulates a family of interchangeable algorithms?
exemple_question.explication: Strategy encapsulates interchangeable algorithms behind a common abstraction.

exemple_question.enonce: What is the healthiest first step before a complex optimization effort?
exemple_question.choix_B: Profile the application to find a real bottleneck
exemple_question.explication: Profiling identifies actual bottlenecks before code is changed.

exemple_question.enonce: What is a direct benefit of low coupling between components?
exemple_question.explication: Low coupling makes components easier to evolve, replace and test independently.

consignes: Each question targets exactly one expected outcome
Difficulty must stay aligned with the target level
The explanation must justify the correct answer
